tickets.title: T-008 — Title-bar git status (branch, ahead/behind, dirty/clean)
spec: `git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD` → branch name
`git status --porcelain` → dirty/clean indicator
`git rev-list --left-right --count @{u}...HEAD` → ahead/behind counts (when upstream exists)

tickets.title: T-005 — Title bar with traffic lights, breadcrumb, model pill (static)
spec: Traffic lights (`-webkit-app-region: drag` parent)
Workspace icon · workspace name · `/` · branch chip · `·` · session title
Right side: model pill (green dot · "Claude Opus 4.7"), context-budget pill ("14,820 / 200k"), no-drag region

tickets.title: T-004 — Three-column layout (sidebar 240 / main 1fr / right rail 320)
spec: Left rail: 240px — Workspace nav, active sessions, user card at bottom
Main: 1fr — conversation pane placeholder, composer placeholder
Right rail: 320px — tab bar (Files / MCP / Context), panel content placeholder

tickets.title: T-003 — Bundle Source Serif 4, Inter Tight, JetBrains Mono locally
spec: Source Serif 4 — italic 8..60 wght 400/500/600, roman 8..60 wght 400/500/600 (display, serif italic is a load-bearing aesthetic choice)
JetBrains Mono — 400/500/600 (code)

tickets.title: T-002 — Port design tokens from atrium.html into tailwind.config.ts
spec: Colors: `bg`, `bg-elev`, `bg-sunk`, `bg-hover`, `ink`, `ink-2/3/4`, `line`, `line-2`, `green`, `green-soft`, `amber`, `amber-soft`, `red`, `red-soft`
Fonts: `serif` (Source Serif 4), `sans` (Inter Tight), `mono` (JetBrains Mono)

tickets.title: T-001 — Tauri scaffold (Vite + React + Tailwind + TypeScript)
spec: `bun tauri dev` launches a native window
Vite HMR works for React changes
Tailwind classes apply

tickets.title: NEW-G — UAT case rollup from acceptance criteria + UAT canvas findings
spec: **Acceptance criteria** — parsed from the ticket description's `## Acceptance` section. Each bullet becomes a UAT case. Status: `pending` until manually marked or until a finding closes it.

**UAT canvas findings** — when a UAT session classifies a finding against this ticket (Wave 4.5 territory, see [STO-2176](https://linear.app/stonekey/issue/STO-2176/uat-canvas-question-card-classified-findings-list)), it lands here as `fail` or `pending` depending on resolution.

**Manually authored UAT cases** — added via the `Generate tests` action on the inline expand (NEW-E) when Claude is asked to author them; persisted in `.atrium/uat-cases.yaml` (or similar) co-located with the project.

tickets.title: NEW-F — Test discovery: auto-find unit tests, count pass/fail/missing per ticket + per wave
spec: Glob: `**/*.test.{ts,tsx,js,jsx}` and `**/*.spec.{ts,tsx}` (configurable per project in CLAUDE.md / atrium config).
Association heuristics, in order:
Test file references the ticket ID in a `// @ticket STO-1234` comment or top-level describe block.

tickets.title: NEW-E — Ticket inline-expand: Spec / Tests / Activity tabs
spec: Three tabs along the top: **Spec** · **Tests** · **Activity**.
Below tabs: panel content (single panel visible at a time).

Footer actions: `Open in canvas` (primary — jumps to Build/Plan canvas with this ticket loaded), `View in Linear` (opens Linear deep link in a separate window or via MCP), `Generate tests` (kicks off Claude to author the missing unit/UAT cases — handed off to /sprint).

tickets.title: W0.6-5 — Click-through to Linear + real ticket detail depth
spec: `TicketRow` gains an `↗` open-in-Linear button → webview posts `openLinear` → host `vscode.env.openExternal(ticket.url)`.
(Optional) `revealBranch` host message — best-effort.

`TicketDetail` Spec / Activity tabs filled with real Linear content (acceptance criteria + comment timeline). Tests tab shows Linear-recorded counts, or "not discovered yet" when `tests.discovered === false`.

tickets.title: W0.6-4 — Spike-gate map + per-wave Pass-N badge
spec: `SpikeGateMap` — renders the wave sequence with ⚠ gate chips on blocked waves ("gated by T-110"). Driven by `board.spikes[]` + each wave's `gatedBy`. Lives in the rollup region.

Per-wave **Pass-N / UAT-loop-back badge** in `WaveSection` — appears only when `wave.passN > 1` (the loop-back machinery, [STO-2177](https://linear.app/stonekey/issue/STO-2177/loop-back-machinery-chip-on-wave-card-return-strip-pass-n-badge)).
Spike-gate map shows a gate chip on each wave whose `gatedBy` is set, naming the spike.

tickets.title: W0.6-3 — Active Work strip (branch → ticket resolution)
spec: `ActiveWork` — collapsible strip showing the active ticket, its branch, state, and a next-action hint.

`resolveActiveTicket(board, branch)` in `board.ts`: match the current git branch name against ticket ids (e.g. branch `…sto-2181…` → [STO-2181](https://linear.app/stonekey/issue/STO-2181/new-a-left-rail-mode-toggle-waves-files-1-resize-handle)); fall back to the first `doing` ticket; else null. The extension host already knows the branch.
Active Work strip shows the ticket matched from the current branch (or the doing fallback).

tickets.title: W0.6-2 — Project rollup bar + segmented progress bars
spec: `RollupBar` component at the top of the cockpit: counts by state (done / doing / todo), spike count, overall progress.
**Segmented state bar** (done/doing/todo as colored segments) — far more scannable than a bare %. Project-level in `RollupBar` and per-wave in `WaveSection` (replaces the current bare % bar).
`computeRollup(board)` and `computeWaveProgress(wave)` in `board.ts`.

tickets.title: W0.6-1 — Board snapshot source + BoardSource loader (replace STUB_WAVES)
spec: `extension/src/atrium-board.json` — committed snapshot of the real Atrium board, generated by a Claude session via the Linear MCP. Schema: `project`, `generatedAt`, `spikes[]`, `waves[]` (name, label, stage, passN, gatedBy, tickets\[\]), each ticket = `id, title, url, priority, state, spec[], tests{passed,failed,missing,discovered}, activity[]`.

`extension/src/board.ts` — pure module (no `vscode` import, mirrors the `cockpit-html.ts` split): `interface BoardSource { load(): Board }`; `SnapshotSource` imports the JSON; `LinearSdkSource` is a stub for the live path.
`extension.ts` `buildInitPayload()` reads from `board.ts` instead of `STUB_WAVES`.

tickets.title: W0.7-3 — Drag-to-reorder priority (Linear sortOrder)
spec: Reorder within a column persists to Linear sortOrder; optimistic + synced/failed/revert.
tsc + vitest green; F5/live-verified.

tickets.title: W0.7-2 — Drag-to-status + optimistic Linear write-back
spec: `useBoardMutations` hook: optimistic state over `init.waves` + per-ticket sync status + reconcile on `init`.

Host `moveTicket {id, toState}` handler → resolve the issue's team workflow-state **id** (todo/doing/review/done → unstarted/started(name\~review)/started/completed; resolved from Linear, cached) → `updateIssue`.
Conflict: read-before-write; if Linear already differs from `fromState`, reject (revert + "changed in Linear" flag), don't clobber.

tickets.title: W0.7-1 — Read-only sprint kanban (spotlight current sprint)
spec: `SprintBoard` + `KanbanCard` (id, title, priority, ↗ open-in-Linear, sync-badge scaffold).
Pure `boardToColumns(wave)` (group tickets by state) + test.
Regenerate `extension/src/atrium-board.json` to match the pruned board (drop cancelled Waves 1/2/3/5/7).

tickets.title: Fast-track gate — UI-detection blocks bypass with warning chip
spec: Acceptance criteria contain any of: `screen`, `canvas`, `UI`, `component`, `dialog`, `modal`, `flow`, `view`, `pane`, `card`
Spec mentions `wireframe`, `mockup`, `design`, `figma`, `prototype`
Wave label includes `UX` or `Design`

tickets.title: Design canvas — mockup-on-canvas + annotation pins
spec: Toolbar: file path (mono), pin count chip (amber soft), edited-timestamp, zoom controls
Stage area: dot-grid background, mockup rendered inside a chrome-styled mini-frame
Numbered annotation pins (amber circles, 24×24) overlay the surface at any pixel

tickets.title: Plan canvas — spec doc + acceptance criteria + ticket-tags
spec: Doc title (28px serif, `-0.025em` letter-spacing)
Doc meta row: date · ticket count · file path
Sections: **Why now** (paragraph), **Acceptance criteria** (checklist with per-criterion `STO-XXXX` ticket-tag column), **Out-of-scope** (paragraph — first-class block; not a footnote)

tickets.title: Wave plan summary on Build — ticket pipeline + UAT phase row
spec: **Heading**: wave name + meta (`N tickets · ~Xm elapsed · ~Ym remaining`)
**Breakdown legend** (right-aligned): coloured dots for `N done · N live · N next · UAT after`
**Done** — green check, struck-through title, green pill

tickets.title: Audit-trail timeline — Pickup / Plan locked / Phase end / Close
spec: **Filled (green)** — Linear comment exists for that step
**Empty (outline)** — required but missing
**Pickup** — "Starting work. Will update with approach."

tickets.title: Pre-flight gate — entry checks for Build / Design canvases
spec: **MCP** — ping Linear if enabled, plus any other configured servers (6/6 ok)
**Git** — `git status` clean + branch naming convention (`feat/<ticket-id>-<desc>`)
**Type-check** — `tsc --noEmit` passes

tickets.title: Session Status rail — persistent across all Tier-2 canvases
spec: **Branch** — name + dirty/clean dot (`feat/<ticket-id>-<desc>` validation)
**Ahead** — unpushed commit count (amber if > 0)
**tsc** — last typecheck result (`passes` / `N errors`)

tickets.title: Loop-back machinery — chip on wave card + return-strip + Pass-N badge
spec: **Amber** `Pass N` chip on the wave card in Tier-1 pipeline
**One-line return-strip** below the pipeline citing the originating finding ("Wave 3 returned to Design 12 minutes ago — UAT-091 reopened the preview height. 2 pins active. Pass 2 of 3")
`Pass N · from <stage>` chip in the wave-title-row's stage-tag area on the Tier-2 canvas

tickets.title: UAT canvas — Question card + classified findings list
spec: **Hero**: Question card (NEW-11 — supports Tab-to-add-note + screenshot)
**Findings table** below the card:
Columns: ID · screenshot thumb (36×26) · description · verdict pill · shot count · next-action

tickets.title: Screenshot annotation — capture, draw, redact, file as finding
spec: Global hotkey `⌘⇧4` triggers a snip overlay (system-style cross-hair selection). Screenshot capture goes via the extension host (Node) or a webview capture; the OS capture path differs under a VS Code extension — flag to revisit. The annotation UI stays in the webview.
Camera button in titlebar / composer attach-row triggers same flow
Selected region opens in an annotation canvas (webview view / editor-tab surface)

tickets.title: Tier-1 Project Overview pipeline (Plan / Design / UX / Build / UAT / Release)
spec: Title row: swatch (stage colour) · name · count
1px border-bottom rule beneath title
Wave card stack below
